title: ‘Moana’ Faces Box Office Test, Less Than Two Years After Franchise’s Last Big Theatrical Splash
full_text: × Skip to main content By Ryan Gajewski Plus Icon Ryan Gajewski Senior Entertainment Reporter View All July 9, 2026 6:42pm Dwayne Johnson in Disney's live-action 'Moana.' Courtesy of Everett Collection Dwayne Johnson and Catherine Laga’aia lead Disney ‘s live-action Moana , which aims to make waves in its box office debut this weekend but could face a challenge, coming less than two years after the latest entry in the island-centric film franchise. Director Thomas Kail ‘s movie is eyeing a domestic weekend start between $60 million and $65 million with a launch spanning 3,900 theaters in North America. This is dialed back from last month, when tracking for the film — which marks the studio’s latest live-action reimagining after striking it big with 2025’s Lilo & Stitch — suggested Moana’ s domestic weekend debut could reach as high as $75 million or above. Related Stories Movies 'Freddy the 13th' Animated Horror-Comedy From Dan Trachtenberg Sets 2028 Release TV 'The Cheetah Girls: Next Gen' Movie Greenlit at Disney; Raven-Symoné, Adrienne Bailon Return Alongside New Cast With a hefty production budget of $250 million, Disney will need Moana to serve as a family-friendly summer option for weeks to come. The film remakes directors John Musker and Ron Clements’ 2016 animated musical of the same name that introduced Johnson as the voice of demigod Maui alongside Auli’i Cravalho as Moana. The movie centers on Moana teaming up with Maui to stop a curse from impacting her family’s island. The new Moana hasn’t quite gone over swimmingly with the majority of critics. In his review for The Hollywood Reporter , chief film critic David Rooney wrote that “this charming new iteration stands confidently on its own,” but the latest Moana holds just a 37 percent Rotten Tomatoes approval rating from reviewers. Disney will hope that audiences aren’t suffering from franchise overload, given that Moana 2 , the animated sequel that saw the returns of Johnson and Cravalho, hit theaters in November 2024. Despite initially being conceived as a Disney+ series, the movie was a smash hit, collecting $225 million domestically over the five-day Thanksgiving frame en route to surpassing $1 billion globally. With the original Moana having hit theaters less than a decade ago — when it scored an $82 million five-day domestic opening over Thanksgiving in 2016 and $643 global cume — this makes it the most recent Disney animated film to get the live-action treatment. Catherine Laga’aia in Moana. Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures/Courtesy Everett Collection “To be honest with you, I never bought into this idea [of], ‘You have to wait 20 years. You have you wait 30 years. It’s too soon,'” Johnson told THR at this week’s Moana premiere in Los Angeles. “I honestly never did — and not because I’m biased and I made the film — but because there’s themes and values in this, in animated Moana , that could translate really well if you saw a real young girl going through it.” Disney’s live-action The Lion King from 2019 remains the biggest success story of the studio’s reimaginings, having opened to $191 million domestically. Lilo & Stitch wasn’t too far behind that with its May 2025 opening of $146 million in North America, en route to hitting $1 billion globally. Chris Sanders, who directed the 2002 animated Lilo & Stitch and voices the titular alien, returns to helm a forthcoming live-action sequel , while a live-action Tangled , starring Teagan Croft and Milo Manheim, has begun production in Spain. A recent misfire for the live-action properties was Snow White , featuring Rachel Zegler and Gal Gadot. This one opened to $42 million domestically in March of last year. Moana has been quite a prosperous brand for the studio. Disney touts that the property has led to more than 1.5 billion hours streamed on Disney+, in addition to selling more than 22 million toys sold and surpassing 26 billion music streams. Maia Kealoha as Lilo in Disney’s live-action Lilo & Stitch. Courtesy of Disney Other family-friendly titles providing competition for the frame include Disney’s Toy Story 5 , which has crossed $800 million globally just ahead of its fourth weekend, and Universal’s Minions & Monsters . The latter opened last weekend to the lowest domestic sum for a title in the Despicable Me franchise. This summer’s ups and downs at the box office suggest that viewers may be more likely to gravitate to a property that has had a longer theatrical absence. DC Studios’ Supergirl became one of the season’s biggest misfires when Warner Bros. released it June 26, a year after Superman landed in cinemas. Additionally, Minions & Monsters arrived two years after 2024’s Despicable Me 4 , which followed 2022’s Minions: The Rise of Gru . Also hitting theaters in wide release is Warner Bros.’ horror offering Evil Dead Burn , the latest installment in the Evil Dead film franchise that kicked off with Sam Raimi’s 1981 original. Rooney’s review for THR noted the new movie’s “orgiastic slaughter.” Looming on the horizon is the July 17 release of Universal’s The Odyssey , with Christopher Nolan directing a star-studded cast that includes Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Robert Pattinson, Anne Hathaway and Lupita Nyong’o. Ready to swing into theaters on July 31 is Sony’s Spider-Man: Brand New Day , also starring Holland. No wide releases are scheduled for the weekend in between those two attention grabbers, giving audiences a chance to catch up on titles like Moana . THR

title: Jennifer Garner On Why It’s “So Important” To Film In LA “As Much As Possible”
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn ‘Teenage Sex and Death At Camp Miasma’ Director & Cast Set ‘Secretary’ Live Reading Dan Trachtenberg’s Untitled ‘Freddy the 13th’ Animated Horror Comedy Gets Release Date ‘Big Brother’s Levi Banks Explains Dropping Out Before Season 28: “Not The Right Fit” View All July 9, 2026 6:29pm Jennifer Garner in 'The Five Star Weekend' Seacia Pavao/Peacock Although Jennifer Garner takes audiences on a trip in The Five-Star Weekend , she prefers staying in Los Angeles for work. The Golden Globe winner recently discussed why it’s been “so important” to her that she works in Hollywood “as much as possible,” while state legislators push to uncap California’s film and TV tax incentives program. “I started really honing in on working in Los Angeles as much as possible,” she explained to the Associated Press . “It can’t always happen. But we’re losing the film industry here. It’s so important to me. I love the crew members in Los Angeles. I grew up with them for the last 30 years.” Related Stories News ‘The Five Star Weekend’ Trailer: Jennifer Garner Plays A Widowed Food Blogger In Upcoming Peacock Adaptation News Election Hail Mary: Karen Bass Copies Mayoral Rival Nithya Raman’s Uncapped Tax Credits Advocacy; Against Paramount-WBD Merger Unless No “Massive Job Losses” Garner continued, “It’s so important to keep filmmaking here in LA. And also, for all of us, not just for me, but for all of us, who have kids, and families, and lives, and homes, and gardens to tend to, we want to be here to watch them all grow.” Watch on Deadline In 2025, on-location production in Los Angeles declined 16.1% , with a slight uptick in Q4 thanks to the California Film & TV Tax Credit Program giving the state’s industry a much-needed infusion. Jennifer Garner says it's important to "keep filmmaking here in LA." She stars in the Peacock series "The Five-Star Weekend." #JenniferGarner #TheFiveStarWeekend pic.twitter.com/yD4qjy3ihA — The Associated Press (@AP) July 9, 2026 Bringing work back to Hollywood has become a major platform for both incumbent Mayor Karen Bass and opponent Nithya Raman, with both calling for California’s $750 million film and TV tax incentives program to be uncapped. Based on the 2023 novel by Elin Hilderbrand, The Five-Star Weekend filmed in both Los Angeles and Nantucket last year. The eight-episode series stars Garner as food blogger Hollis Shaw, who hosts a weekend with her friends at her Nantucket home following the death of her husband. Developed for Peacock by Bekah Brunstetter, the series also stars Chloë Sevigny, Regina Hall, Gemma Chan, D’Arcy Carden, Harlow Jane and Timothy Olyphant. title: USMNT’s World Cup Exit Scores Historic Ratings With 33M Viewers – Update
full_text: Skip to main content By Peter White Peter White Executive Editor, Television @peterzwhite More Stories By Peter USMNT’s World Cup Exit Scores Historic Ratings With 33M Viewers – Update Ex-Pineapple Street Pair Launch New Podcast Shingle, Set Projects With Dan Taberski, Amy Poehler & Patrick Radden Keefe TV Academy President Maury McIntyre Talks Emmy Noms, Legacy Award, How Multiple Late-Night Shows Might Win & A Pending TV Rights Deal View All July 9, 2026 6:03pm Christian Pulisic Ercin Erturk/Getty Images UPDATE 7/9 : The final viewership numbers for the U.S.-Belgium match are in, and they are the biggest for any telecast since the Super Bowl. The Round of 16 game was watched by 33.086 million viewers — the most ever for a soccer telecast in the U.S. and the largest non-NFL audience on any network in a decade, since Game 7 of the World Series on Nov. 2 2016. The viewership peaked to 41M from 9:15 pm-9:30 pm ET. PREVIOUS 7/7 : The U.S. defeat by Belgium in the last 16 of the World Cup helped Fox keep breaking ratings records for soccer coverage. The USMNT ’s 4-1 loss to the Europeans was watched by 30M viewers, becoming the most-watched soccer telecast in U.S. history. It hit a peak of 36.9M viewers between 9:15-9:30 pm ET during the start of the second half as Mauricio Pochettino’s team was chasing an equalizer. Related Stories Casting 'Baywatch' Adds Blair Redford To Recurring Cast News As Top Streamers Battle Churn, Emerging AI Start-Up Versus Offers Ways To Hold On To Viewers It’s been a huge World Cup for Fox, which already broke soccer ratings records in the last round with the USMNT’s win over Bosnia and Herzegovina becoming the most-watched soccer telecast in U.S. history before the Belgium game. That match was watched by 24.4M and peaked with nearly 32M viewers. Watch on Deadline Belgium went ahead in the first half thanks to a Charles De Ketelaere goal. Malik Tillman equalized in the 31st minute but Belgium were back on top two minutes later thanks to De Ketelaere again. Hans Vanaken made it three thanks to an awful goalkeeping error before Romelu Lukaku put a fourth in in stoppage time. The U.S. exit will be a blow to Fox, but it has been a huge tournament for the rights holders, thanks in part due to the success of the USMNT, as well as co-hosts Mexico and Canada, as well as some upsets and close games. Fox ‘s Mike Mulvihill, president of insights and analytics for Fox’s entertainment and sports divisions as well as Tubi, told Deadline that the reach of the World Cup is “almost like having a second NFL season”. “There really shouldn’t be any debate at this point about whether soccer is popular or will ever be popular,” he added. “I guess the question now is just, what does it mean for the future of soccer?” Marco Bassetti & Jeff Zucker On $8B Mega-Merger; Rights Arm Plan Roeg Sutherland, Vincent Maraval & More Backing New Toronto Sales Market Is Oscar & Palme d’Or Winner Neon Lining Up UK Distribution? ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Swings Onto Tracking With ’26-Best $180M+ Read More About: Comments

title: How Olivia Rodrigo Is Getting Back at the Trump Administration
full_text: × Skip to main content By Kevin Dolak Plus Icon Kevin Dolak View All July 9, 2026 4:52pm Olivia Rodrigo Nick Walker/Geffen Pop sensation Olivia Rodrigo is mobilizing voters and buoying turnout for the midterm elections this year, which may be a bit of payback against the Trump administration after one of her songs was used in one of its anti-immigration advertisements. In November, Rodrigo hit back at the Department of Homeland Security after her song, “all-american bitch,” was used in an advertisement that encouraged immigrants living in the U.S. without proper documentation to self-deport. The Instagram post, made in collaboration with the White House’s account, used the singer’s work as it showed images of people of color being detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents. Related Stories TV Fox Nation Is About to Premiere a Documentary on the UFC White House Event TV John Oliver Likens the Current Condition of D.C. Reflecting Pool to Sex With the Grinch: "Green, Smelly" “Don’t ever use my songs to promote your racist, hateful propaganda,” replied Rodrigo — who is of Filipino descent, with a great-great-grandfather who emigrated from the Philippines — in the comments on the Instagram post at the time. Last month, to Da z ed magazine, she elaborated on the topic: “I was just scrolling on my phone. It was so deeply disturbing to see that propaganda, and the fact it was my song in there made me feel even more enraged. What they’re doing is so awful and barbaric and cruel. I am really sad to be in a country that thinks that’s OK.” The Dazed interview wasn’t the only time Rodrigo aired her frustration. On the cover of British Vogue , she revisited the episode, describing the administration’s use of her music as a “weaponization” of her art, calling the whole ordeal “dystopian.” While the “good 4 u” singer was vocal about her outrage, it seems that her true revenge is now being served — cold and without even dropping a name. Rodrigo is helping launch a sweepstakes offering tickets to her Daisy Chain Fields festival , encouraging entrants to check their voter registration status, register, or explore election resources for a chance at an all-expenses-paid trip to the festival — travel and hotel included — according to HeadCount, the nonprofit running the promotion. HeadCount partners with musicians and music events nationwide to build awareness around civil rights and voter participation, working at over 1,000 concerts and festivals a year. The festival, featuring only women artists, has tapped Chappell Roan, Doechii, Santigold, Sarah McLachlan, Mitski, The Breeders, Bikini Kill and Stevie Nicks to perform. It will take place Aug. 29 in Irvine, California, and all net proceeds will be directed toward charities for women and girls. And it’s shaping up to be more than a mere concert. Beyond the all-women lineup, organizers say Daisy Chain Fields will fold in an activism component, with nonprofits on-site sharing resources on issues like reproductive rights, maternal health, economic empowerment and domestic violence prevention. Partners reportedly include the Center for Reproductive Rights and Planned Parenthood, turning the festival grounds into as much a civic showcase as a stage. THR

title: George Lucas’ New L.A. Museum Will Offer Free Access to Neighbors
full_text: × Skip to main content By Gary Baum Plus Icon Gary Baum Senior Writer garymbaum Follow Sign Up View All July 9, 2026 4:27pm Lucas Museum of Narrative Art Courtesy of Lucas Museum of Narrative Art Los Angeles’ Lucas Museum of Narrative Art , opening to the public Sept. 22, has announced that it’s offering free annual passes as well as an early preview to its closest residential neighbors in South L.A.’s 90037 ZIP code. George Lucas and his partner Mellody Hobson ’s long-awaited $1 billion, 300,000-square-foot cultural institution — with its fittingly futuristic building, given that it’s made possible by the Star Wars fortune — will feature more than 1,300 objects on display, as well as 11 acres of landscaped space across its campus. “We wanted to make sure the neighbors that have been seeing the museum built from the ground up for the past eight years are the first ones inside,” explains the museum’s CEO Tracey Bates of the renewable pass program , which allows holders to reserve a pair of tickets. Related Stories Movies 'Minions & Monsters' Review: Antic Blend of Silliness and Cinephilia Delights, Until It Doesn't Movies George Lucas Cast in 'Minions & Monsters' After Revealing Love for Minions According to census data, the 3-square-mile 90037 ZIP code includes more than 17,000 households. A quarter of the predominantly Hispanic and African American population lives below the poverty line. The Lucas Museum resides at the ZIP code’s northern boundary, within Exposition Park, which also features the California Science Center as well as L.A. County’s Natural History Museum. The renowned landscape designer Mia Lehrer — whose other projects include SoFi Stadium — is behind the Lucas Museum’s grounds, which complement Ma Yansong’s otherworldly architecture. “Our gardens and public spaces are really their backyard now,” Bates notes of the local community. “We want to give them reasons to visit.” The museum’s collection, which boasts the original Lucasfilm archives retained by Lucas after the company’s 2012 sale to Disney for $4 billion, features a pantheon ranging from Norman Rockwell, Jack Kirby and Robert Crumb to Frida Kahlo and the local star Judith Baca, best known for her monumental mural of local history along the Los Angeles River in the San Fernando Valley. The Lucas Museum’s arrival follows the high-profile debut in April of LACMA’s David Geffen Galleries. It opens as the city’s La Brea Tar Pits and Museum has closed for renovations. THR

title: ‘Baywatch’ Reboot Cast: Meet the New Stars (and Four Returning Faces) in Red Suits
full_text: Skip to main content By Lexi Carson Plus Icon Lexi Carson Associate Editor View All July 9, 2026 4:10pm Published on July 9, 2026 Stephen Amell, Shay Mitchell, Noah Beck and Livvy Dunne Matt Winkelmeyer/Getty Image; Gilbert Flores/Variety/Getty Images; Rodin Eckenroth/Getty Images; Anna Webber/Getty Images Logo text Red bathing suits. Slow-motion running. Saving lives. All are returning for Baywatch. A reboot of the original lifeguard drama , which ran for 11 seasons starting in 1989 and an ensemble including David Hasselhoff, Pamela Anderson, Carmen Electra and Jason Momoa, is headed to Fox. The 12-episode series is slated to premiere in January 2027. With production underway in Venice Beach and on the Fox studio lot in Century City, The Hollywood Reporter is rounding up the names audiences can expect to see in this new generation of Baywatch. Matt Nix ( Burn Notice, True Lies ) serves as showrunner and executive producer alongside McG ( The O.C., Charlie’s Angels ), Michael Berk, Greg Bonann, Doug Schwartz, Dante Di Loreto and Mike Horowitz. McG will also direct the first episode. Read on to find out who is appearing. This list was first published on April 4 and has been updated to include more castmembers as they’re announced. Stephen Amell Image Credit: Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images Logo text Stephen Amell was the first official cast member announced to join Baywatch. He will play the lead role of Hobie Buchannon, son of Mitch Buchannon (Hasselhoff), who is a Baywatch Captain. Amell previously appeared in Suits LA , Arrow, Heels , The Flash, The Borderline, Code 8 and Legends of Tomorrow. He is represented by UTA, Leverage Management and Sloane, Offer, Weber & Dern. Jessica Belkin Image Credit: Daniel Poplawsky Logo text Jessica Belkin portrays Charlie Vale , the biological daughter of Amell’s Hobie Buchannon, in a series regular role. Charlie leaves her complicated home life in Texas and shows up on her father’s doorstep with the hope of becoming a Baywatch lifeguard. Belkin’s credits include The Hunting Wives , Bad Day and Last Straw. The actress will also appear in the upcoming Legally Blonde prequel series Elle . She is represented by Paradigm, Brillstein Entertainment Partners, Schuller Talent Mgmt and Stewart Brookman. Hassie Harrison Image Credit: JC Olivera/Variety/Getty Images Logo text Hassie Harrison plays series regular Nat, a former foster kid turned Olympic athlete who has high standards when it comes to lifeguarding. She is Hobie’s closest friend and loyal right hand. She appeared in Taylor Sheridan’s Yellowstone, as well as in Hart of Dixie, The Iron Orchard, Tacoma FD and Dangerous Animals. Harrison is repped by Gersh, Range Media Partners, and Johnson Shapiro Slewett & Kole. Noah Beck Image Credit: Jerod Harris/Getty Images Logo text Noah Beck is a series regular playing Luke, a rookie lifeguard, who is the youngest in a family of firefighters who prefers the beach over the firehouse. He looks up to Amell’s Hobie as a mentor. Beck began his career as a social media influencer before transitioning into acting. He is known for his role in Tubi’s YA romance franchise Sidelined . James Van Der Beek played Beck’s father in Sidelined 2, which was his final onscreen film role . He is repped by UTA, Underscore Talent, and Goodman, Genow, Schenkman. Shay Mitchell Image Credit: The Collective You Logo text Shay Mitchell joined the cast as a series regular . She plays Trina, a former lawyer who left a top-tier firm to become a full-time lifeguard. The actress and entrepreneur starred as Emily Fields on all seven seasons of Pretty Little Liars . Mitchell has also appeared in Netflix’s You and Hulu’s Dollface, as well as in film credits for Mother’s Day, Dreamland and Something from Tiffany’s . She founded and serves as the chief creative officer of the travel and lifestyle brand Béis. Mitchell is represented by Linden Entertainment, WME and Grubman Shire Meiselas & Sacks. Thaddeus LaGrone Image Credit: Courtesy of Subject Logo text Thaddeus LaGrone plays Brad, who served two tours in an elite division of the Marines before returning home to Venice Beach to take care of his father. LaGrone is a musician and former football player. He recently began acting and landed roles in the upcoming Hulu NFL drama series The Land from Dan Fogelman, and in Jonathan Levine’s Mr. Irrelevant opposite Michael Shannon and David Corenswet. He is repped by Brave Artists Management and Goodman, Genow, Schenkman, Smelkinson & Christopher. David Chokachi Image Credit: Gilbert Flores/Variety/Getty Images; Pearson Television/Courtesy Everett Collection Logo text David Chokachi will reprise his role of Cody Madison. He played the original series’ character from seasons six through nine. Cody is a lifeguard who also runs The Shoreline, the unofficially official Baywatch bar-and-grill. Madison was the second cast member announced to join the reboot. He’s also appeared in Witchblade (2001-2002), Soul Surfer (2011) and Emerald Run (2020). Chokachi is represented by LINK Entertainment and Singular Talent. Erika Eleniak Image Credit: Gilbert Flores/Variety/Getty Images; Pearson All-American Television/Courtesy Everett Collection Logo text Another OG Baywatch cast member is heading back to the beach. Erika Eleniak, who played Shauni McClain in the first two seasons before exiting at the start of season three, is set to return as a guest star in the reboot. Per Fox, “Former Baywatch lifeguard turned Santa Monica city councilwoman Shauni McClain returns to the beach to help Hobie kick off the annual ‘Beach Games’ between reigning champions of Baywatch versus the Coast Guard.” The actress began her career as a child with a role in Steven Spielberg’s E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982) and joined Baywatch shortly after high school. She later appeared in Under Siege, The Beverly Hillbillies, Chasers, A Pyromaniac’s Love Story , Bordello of Blood, CSI: Miami and Desperate Housewives. Eleniak is represented by Danielle Bilodeau at The Characters Talent Agency. Livvy Dunne Image Credit: Courtesy of Talent Logo text Olivia “Livvy” Dunne is making her professional acting debut in Baywatch. Dunne will be a recurring star, playing Grace, an enthusiastic junior lifeguard. A former LSU gymnast, she gained a large social media following by documenting her life in sports and beyond. She made her Sports Illustrated Swimsuit debut in 2023 and returned as a cover star in 2025. Dunne is represented by Brillstein Entertainment Partners and The Team. Brooks Nader Image Credit: Gilbert Flores/Variety/Getty Images Logo text Brooks Nader plays Selene, a Zuma Beach lifeguard who has tension with Amell’s Hobie. As described by Fox, the “sharp-tongued” captain is “never on good terms, their relationship only goes downhill after Hobie brings his daughter Charlie onto his team… and it becomes clear that their rivalry goes much deeper than lifeguarding.” Nader is a reality star known for appearing on Love Thy Nader and Dancing With the Stars. She is also a swimsuit model who wore the classic Baywatch one-piece on the cover of the 2023 Sports Illustrated Swimsuit issue. Nader is represented by WME, Dixon Talent, Inc. and Leigh Fidler. Luke Eisner Image Credit: Frazer Harrison/Getty Images Logo text Luke Eisner plays Koya, a recurring character who’s a rookie and best friends with Beck’s Luke. The actor is best known for starring as the romantic lead in Netflix’s original film Tall Girl and its 2022 sequel. He is also a model who has worked with Calvin Klein and Ralph Lauren. Eisner is repped by Buchwald and Thruline Entertainment. Nadia Gray Image Credit: Frazer Harrison/Getty Images Logo text Nadia Gray is a recurring guest star who plays Lisa, an EMT and partner to the team of lifeguards on Venice Beach. Gray’s previous credits include Bright, All Rise, Sandy Wexler and Days of Our Lives. She is represented by Adrian Hamerski at Brave Artists Management and lawyer Adam Vitabile at Hirsch Wallerstein Hayum Matlof and Fishman. Charlie McElveen Image Credit: Tommaso Boddi/Getty Images Logo text Charlie McElveen will play Ethan, a former colleague of Mitchell’s Trina. He is a successful lawyer who questions her recent career changes. McElveen has appeared in The Creator, Clipped, Pretty Little Liars: Original Sin and High Potential. He is represented by Framework Entertainment and CESD Talent Agency. Ashley Moore Image Credit: Hutton Supancic/SXSW Conference & Festivals/Getty Images Logo text Ashley Moore plays rookie lifeguard Jasmine, who has a lot of skills but holds the reputation of being the mean girl of the beach. The actress is best known for her role in the Amazon TV series I Know What You Did Last Summer (2021). She’s also recognized for Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping (2016), Tripped Up (2023), Rule of Thirds (2024) and Festival of the Living Dead (2024). Moore is represented by Gersh, Luber Roklin and Skrzyniarz & Mallean. Kylar Miranda Image Credit: Nick Jensen Logo text Kylar Miranda plays Diego, an experienced lifeguard from East L.A. and the Baywatch team’s handyman. Miranda is known for roles in On Swift Horses (2024), Mayans M.C. ( 2018) and Minx (2022). He is represented by LA Talent and MRK MGMT. Mary McDonnell Image Credit: Alberto Rodriguez/Variety/Getty Images Logo text Mary McDonnell plays a recurring guest star, Gayle, the mother of Hobie and grandmother of Charlie. McDonnell’s acting credits include Battlestar Galactica, Grey’s Anatomy, ER , Independence Day, Donnie Darko and Grand Canyon , among others. She is currently appearing in Netflix’s The Boroughs, executive produced by the Duffer Brothers. She is repped by Innovative Artists and Anonymous Content and Wallman PR. Michael Bergin Image Credit: Gilbert Flores/Variety/Getty Images; Courtesy Everett Collection Logo text Michael Bergin is returning to the beach. Bergin, who starred in the original series as lifeguard Jack “J.D.” Darius, is set to reprise his role as a guest star in the reboot. Bergin has also been a series lead in the TV movie Baywatch: Hawaiian Wedding , alongside David Hasselhoff, Pamela Anderson and Carmen Electra. His additional credits include Charmed, CSI: Miami, Starforce and The Broken Hearts Club. He is also known for famously dating Carolyn Bessette before she married John F. Kennedy Jr. Bergin was portrayed by Noah Fearnley in this year’s FX series Love Story . He is represented by The Chasin Agency. Kelly Packard Image Credit: Benjamin McDonnell; Pearson All-American Television/Courtesy Everett Collection Logo text Kelly Packard is another original Baywatch lifeguard, April Giminski, coming back for the reboot as a guest star. The actress has also appeared in Ripley’s Believe It or Not! , California Dreams, Stalked by My Neighbor and Better Things. Packard is repped by Rafael Villegas at Kim Dawson Agency. John Beckwith Image Credit: Manoli Figetakis/Getty Images Logo text John Beckwith has joined the series as a recurring character. Beckwith plays Julian, a Ph.D. student on an ocean research team brought in to help Baywatch. After being rescued by Charlie (Belkin), sparks fly between the pair. Beckwith’s credits have included Feud, Three Women, Grind and most recently Song Sung Blue, which starred Kate Hudson and Hugh Jackman. He is repped by Innovative and Linden Entertainment. Blair Redford Image Credit: Dave Kotinsky/Getty Images for STARZ Logo text Blair Redford plays Hunter, a cameraman who has worked everywhere from Hollywood film sets to war zones. While filming a news special on female first responders, he meets the Baywatch team, and his chemistry with one of the lifeguards convinces him to stay in town. According to Fox, Redford’s role is heavily recurring. The actor’s previous credits include Marvel’s The Gifted , The Young and the Restless , Passions , The Lying Game , Switched at Birth , Satisfaction , Unsinkable and Three Women , in which he appeared alongside fellow cast member John Beckwith. Redford is repped by IAG and AWA. THR

title: Joy Sunday Manifested Working With Jason Bateman, Then ‘DTF St. Louis’ Earned Her Her First Emmy Nomination
full_text: × Skip to main content By Naomi Jeffries Plus Icon Naomi Jeffries Intern View All July 9, 2026 3:46pm Joy Sunday as Agent Jodie Plumb in 'DTF St. Louis.' HBO Joy Sunday is officially Emmy-nominated. The actress earned her first Emmy nomination in the supporting actress category for her performance as Agent Jodie Plumb in DTF St. Louis , the acclaimed limited series, which also landed 13 nominations, including for best limited or anthology series, when 2026 Emmy nominations were announced July 8. Speaking with The Hollywood Reporter shortly after nominations were announced, Sunday says she learned the news alongside her castmates. Related Stories TV 'Love Island USA' Host Ariana Madix Reacts to First Emmy Nomination: "I'm So Over the Moon" TV 'Dancing With the Stars' Bosses, Dancers Celebrate Breaking Back Into Emmys Race: "Deeply Grateful for This Recognition" “It was all happening very quickly,” Sunday recalls. “Gosh, I was actually on the phone with [series creator] Steve [Conrad]’s assistant/co-producers when it all happened, and then my phone just kind of rang up, and the entire cast is all in the group chat with the producers, so pretty much everyone [found out] at the same time. It really speaks to the kind of family feel we had on set. We were all just celebrating together.” Sunday’s been seen on major series such as Netflix’s Emmy-winning supernatural comedy Wednesday and Blank Slate Pictures’ Yas Kween, but it was DTF St. Louis that would land her first acting Emmy nomination — and she wasn’t surprised. “I really have so much faith in this show. It’s an honor and a gift to be recognized for my work on it, but amongst everything, it’s the fact that everybody’s gotten recognition that’s just so beautiful; I’m really happy for everyone,” says Sunday. “Working on the set was my dream job. Of course, there’s so much that makes that meaningful, but to be recognized by the [TV] Academy on top of it was just the cream of the crop.” Sunday wasn’t the only DTF castmember to be recognized, as co-stars Linda Cardellini , Richard Jenkins , David Harbour and Jason Bateman also received acting nominations. Sharing scenes with the latter was a full-circle moment for Sunday, who somewhat manifested the opportunity before she even auditioned for the HBO series. “My first day working with him was probably about a month into shooting. I had flown in on a red-eye from Wednesday , had no sleep, and was about to spend a straight week interrogating Jason Bateman,” she says with a laugh. “And it was seamless. It speaks to how easygoing and warm the energy on set was. Working opposite Jason and Richard Jenkins just all came together. I’m so grateful to both of them for being such fantastic scene partners.” She adds, “Almost a year to the date of when I auditioned, I had texted my manager, ‘I want to shadow Jason Bateman,’ and, in a sense, I ended up doing that.” Although she left most of Agent Plumb’s wardrobe behind when filming for the series wrapped, Sunday didn’t leave the set entirely empty-handed. “That was very much a Christmas came early kind of set. There are sweaters, T-shirts, keychains. I think I made Richard some golf balls that say ‘Indiana Jones Jack Off Classics.’ There are so many different mementos from that. I’ve got a hat here. There are so many things that I’ve kept,” Sunday admits. “I mean, my wardrobe was fabulous, but I didn’t take any of it,” she adds, laughing. Reflecting on building Agent Plumb, Sunday says, above all, she wanted the special crimes investigator to lead with compassion. “I did want to infuse a lot of warmth and empathy into this person who is taking a very holistic approach to how she’s going about this investigation, so kind of straddling between the two was a really exciting challenge for me,” says Sunday. “Getting to do that opposite Richard Jenkins was just — I can’t even put into words how amazing that was.” THR
